underlying: BLS Unemployment Rate, ages 16-24, seasonally adjusted (FRED LNS14024887, BLS Employment Situation Table A-10)
method: RULES VERIFIED via Kalshi API. Market resolves YES if ANY 2026 Employment Situation release reports LNS14024887 < 7.5% (early-close lookback over 8 remaining 2026 releases: May, Jun, Jul, Aug, Sep, Oct, Nov, and Dec 4 release of Nov data). The previous theo had this INVERTED (assumed YES = above). Recompute as P(min over remaining 2026 prints < 7.5). Latest series prints (SA): Mar26 8.5, Feb26 9.5, Jan26 ~9.0, Dec25 ~9.2, Nov25 ~9.4, Oct25 ~9.5, with 2025 summer peak ~10.8 in July (NSA peak even higher; SA pattern decoupled). Monthly first-difference std on this 16-24 SA series ~0.55-0.70pp (much noisier than agg U3 because of small sample). Modeling: assume per-release X_t ~ N(mu_t, 0.65) with mu_t mildly drifting from 8.5 (Apr release of Mar data already in) flat-to-soft toward 8.6-8.8 by Q4. Single-release P(X < 7.5) = Phi((7.5-8.6)/0.65) ~= 4.5%. With 8 monthly draws and intra-series autocorrelation (~0.5), effective independent draws ~3.5, so P(min < 7.5) ~= 1 - (1-0.045)^3.5 ~= 14.7%.
notes: MAJOR REVISION: prior theo had rules inverted. Confirmed via Kalshi API call to /trade-api/v2/markets/KXYOUTHUN-26DEC04-T7.5: 'market resolves YES if the unemployment rate for ages 16 to 24, seasonally adjusted, is below 7.5% in any Employment Situation release published during 2026'. Source = BLS series LNS14024887 (16-24 SA). Early-close on first sub-7.5 print. YES is now BELOW (not above), and it is a path-dependent min-of-8 lookback. Market YES @ 14-15c is essentially fairly priced vs my model 15%; no edge either side. Sources: ALT-DATA cross-checks: (1) ADP National Employment Report releases monthly with ~2-week lead vs BLS; April ADP NER drops May 6 and historically correlates with subsequent BLS 16-24 moves; (2) JOLTS quits/hires age cohorts on lag; (3) Indeed Hiring Lab age-cohort job postings (younger-skewed sectors leading); (4) weekly BLS state LAUS for trend confirmation. Risks for the market resolving YES (i.e., a sub-7.5 print): if labor market unexpectedly tightens OR if a single noisy CPS small-sample print prints 1+pp below trend (has occurred ~3x in past 5 years on this series); summer months tend to print SA-consistent with year, but small-sample volatility on 16-24 SA is the main YES driver. Confidence raised to HIGH because rules now confirmed and model is well-calibrated to market. Previous 88% was a directional error; correct probability is ~15%.
